title: abies marocana subsp. tazaotana
intro: Abies marocana subsp. tazaotana, also known as Moroccan fir, is a subspecies of Abies marocana, a coniferous tree native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. The resin and essential oils extracted from this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Abies marocana subsp. tazaotana include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's resin is often used to create infused oils, salves, and teas, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
